info.name: Check Point MCP Agent (AWS AgentCore)
info.description:
Two ways to ask the hosted security-ops agent a question.

1. **Bridge (Bearer token)** — simplest. Provision once with `python3 -m chkpmcpaws bridge provision`, then fill the `bridgeUrl` and `bridgeToken` collection variables (`python3 -m chkpmcpaws bridge show --reveal-token` prints both).

2. **Direct SigV4** — no bridge needed; calls the AgentCore runtime API directly. Set the `awsAccessKey`/`awsSecretKey` (and `awsSessionToken` for temporary credentials) variables, plus `runtimeArnEncoded` (the runtime ARN, URL-encoded — `bridge show` prints the full URL ready to copy).

Both return JSON: {"result": "...", "usage": {...}, "model": "..."}.

request.description: POST any plain-English question. Optional: "session" groups turns for AgentCore Memory recall; "actor" keeps per-analyst memory.

item.name: Ask the agent — direct SigV4 (no bridge)

request.description: Calls InvokeAgentRuntime directly with AWS Signature v4 (service name: bedrock-agentcore). The session-id header must be 33+ characters.
